title: vicia varia subsp. ambigua
intro: Vicia varia subsp. ambigua, also known as winter vetch or winter vetchling, is a plant species belonging to the Fabaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to its high content of alkaloids and saponins. The health benefits of Vicia varia subsp. ambigua include its potential to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its ability to act as a diuretic and help with urinary issues. Herbal preparations of Vicia varia subsp. ambigua can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are typically used in the form of dried leaves or flowers.
